frameworks.name: population receptive field (pRF) modeling
frameworks.one_line: Voxel-wise estimation of neuronal population receptive field position and spatial extent

frameworks.name: elliptical pRF estimation
frameworks.one_line: Extension claiming many highly elliptical pRFs in early visual cortex

frameworks.name: circular pRF estimation
frameworks.one_line: Conventional isotropic Gaussian pRF used as the validated baseline

core_question: Are population receptive fields in early visual cortex actually highly elliptical or nearly circular?
key_assumptions: pRF aspect ratio is identifiable from fMRI given enough precision
Validated software on ground-truth simulations indexes method reliability
Orientation column scale and map structure constrain plausible aspect ratios
manipulations: Applied prior elliptical-pRF software to circular ground-truth simulations
Re-analyzed an independent dataset with a separately validated software package
Simulated measurement conditions to test sensitivity to aspect ratio
key_findings: Prior elliptical-pRF software fits ellipses to circular ground-truth data
In early visual cortex (V1-V3) aspect ratios are below 2
Current methods cannot reliably discriminate aspect ratio 1.5 from circular
Simulations identify measurement choices that could improve aspect-ratio sensitivity
interpretations: Reports of highly elliptical pRFs reflect software artifact rather than biology
pRFs in early visual cortex are nearly circular, consistent with orientation-column scale
limitations_noted: Empirical and theoretical methods lack precision to detect small ellipticity
connecting_signals: early visual cortex V1 V2 V3
